body:
Hey, just a heads up — you mentioned Sunday that you wanted to get the fall orchid society newsletter draft to the board by Wednesday at the latest. It's Tuesday evening and I figured I'd nudge you since I know how back-to-back your mornings at the clinic get this week.

Also, did you want me to grab Emma from soccer practice tomorrow so you have more time to wrap that up without rushing?

Let me know. — Craig
snippet: Hey, just a heads up — you mentioned Sunday you wanted the fall newsletter draft...
subject: Society newsletter draft reminder

body:
Ugh, thank you — I completely lost track of the day. I drafted most of it this afternoon but still need to finish the show-prep section and the repotting calendar before I can send it to the board. Hoping to finish after Emma's down tonight.

Yes please on the soccer pickup tomorrow, that would genuinely save me. I'll owe you one.

— Jean
snippet: Ugh, thank you — I completely lost track of the day. I drafted most of it this...
subject: Re: Society newsletter draft reminder

body:
Hey Jean! It feels like forever since we've actually sat down together. Are you free any day this week for lunch? I was thinking maybe Thursday or Friday around noon?

There's that new Mediterranean place on Caldwell that everyone keeps talking about — Olive & Stone or something like that. Might be fun to try it out. Let me know what works for you!
snippet: Hey Jean! It feels like forever since we've actually sat down together. Are you ...
subject: Lunch this week?

body:
Dottie! Yes, I've been meaning to reach out — the clinic and show prep have been absolutely swallowing me whole lately. Thursday works perfectly actually, Friday I have a full afternoon of patients I can never seem to get out of.

I've heard good things about Olive & Stone too! Megan from the practice went last month and said the lamb flatbread is incredible. Noon Thursday it is — should I make a reservation or do you think we can just walk in?
snippet: Dottie! Yes, I've been meaning to reach out — the clinic and show prep have...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Thursday noon is perfect! I checked their website and they do take reservations, so I went ahead and booked a table for two just to be safe — it looks like they get pretty packed midday.

Can't wait to hear all about what's been going on with you. And yes, I am absolutely ordering the lamb flatbread based on that recommendation. See you Thursday!

— Dorothy
snippet: Thursday noon is perfect! I checked their website and they do take reservations,...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Hey, I finally got the updated drawings from the contractor. He sent over three layout options for the kitchen — I've been staring at them for an hour and honestly can't decide. Do you have time tonight or tomorrow to sit down and look through them together? I want to get back to him by end of week.

Also, the tile swatches arrived. They're on the kitchen counter if you want to take a look before we talk.
snippet: Hey, I finally got the updated drawings from the contractor. He sent over three ...
subject: Kitchen renovation - contractor layout options

body:
Tonight works! I'll be home by 6:30. Can you forward the PDFs so I can take a quick look before then? I'd rather have some time to form an opinion before we're sitting there on the spot.

I already noticed the tile swatches — the gray one is really nice but I'm worried it might show every speck of dirt. We should talk about that too.
snippet: Tonight works! I'll be home by 6:30. Can you forward the PDFs so I can take a qu...
subject: Re: Kitchen renovation - contractor layout options

body:
Just forwarded them — check your personal email, I sent to that one by mistake. Sorry!

For what it's worth, I'm leaning toward Option B. It moves the island slightly but opens up the walkway a lot. Option C looks great in the rendering but I think it'll feel cramped once the appliances are actually in there.

And yes, the gray tile. I had the same thought. Maybe we ask about a matte finish version?
snippet: Just forwarded them — check your personal email, I sent to that one by mistake. ...
subject: Re: Kitchen renovation - contractor layout options

body:
Got them, thanks. Okay, I've looked through all three and I actually agree with you on Option B — the flow makes a lot more sense. Option A is the safest but it doesn't really change anything we already dislike about the layout.

Let's ask about the matte tile finish tonight and also get clarity on the cabinet lead times before we commit. I don't want another situation where we're locked into a timeline and the materials aren't ready.

See you at 6:30.
snippet: Got them, thanks. Okay, I've looked through all three and I actually agree with ...
subject: Re: Kitchen renovation - contractor layout options

body: Hey, I looked into the drive and it's only about an hour from Upper Arlington if we leave Friday after work. I was thinking we could head out around 5:30 to beat the worst of the rush hour heading south on 33. Does that work for you? Also, did you end up booking the cabin or are we still deciding between the two options you saved?
snippet: Hey, I looked into the drive and it's only about an hour if we leave Friday after...
subject: Trip logistics - Hocking Hills weekend

body:
I went ahead and booked the one near Old Man's Cave — the reviews were better and it had the covered porch and hot tub I know you wanted. Confirmation should be in your email too.

5:30 works but can we push to 6? I have a late patient that day that might run over. If 33 is backed up we can always stop for dinner somewhere around Lancaster and let it clear out.
snippet: I went ahead and booked the one near Old Man's Cave — the reviews were better...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - Hocking Hills weekend

body:
6 is fine. Stopping in Lancaster actually sounds good — there's a diner off 33 I've been wanting to try. I'll look up the address.

Also, do you know if the cabin has good parking? I couldn't tell from the listing and the gravel lots out there can be tight.
snippet: 6 is fine. Stopping in Lancaster actually sounds good — there's a diner off...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - Hocking Hills weekend

body:
Just checked the listing — it says two gravel spots included right by the cabin, so we'll be fine.

I'll pack Saturday night and Sunday morning. Can you handle the cooler and snacks? And don't forget to top off the greenhouse reservoir before we go, the dendrobiums won't last the weekend dry.

Sending you the diner — looks great, I added it to our shared maps list.
snippet: Just checked the listing — it says two gravel spots included right by the cabin...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - Hocking Hills weekend

body:
On it — I'll grab the cooler from the garage tonight and do a snack run Thursday. Greenhouse reservoir is already on my list, I promise.

Just confirmed the diner closes at 9 so we should be fine timing-wise if we leave at 6. Really looking forward to this, feels like it's been forever since we've gotten out of town. See you Friday.

Craig
snippet: On it — I'll grab the cooler from the garage tonight and do a snack run Thursday...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - Hocking Hills weekend

body:
Hey, got a notice in the mail today that our home warranty with ShieldHome is coming up for renewal at the end of the month. The base plan is $589 for the year, same as last time, but they're also offering an upgraded plan with appliance coverage for $749. Given that the dishwasher has been acting up, might be worth considering the upgrade. Can you take a look when you get a chance and let me know what you think before I call them?

Craig
snippet: Hey, got a notice in the mail today that our home warranty with ShieldHome is co...
subject: Home warranty renewal

body:
I actually looked into this a few weeks ago when I saw the renewal was coming up. I think the upgraded plan makes sense — we've already had two service calls this year and the dishwasher issue alone could easily cost more than the $160 difference if it turns out to be a compressor or pump problem.

Go ahead and call them to lock in the upgraded plan. Just make sure they honor last year's deductible rate ($100 per claim) since I saw some reviews saying they've been trying to raise it on renewals. Let me know what they say.

Jean
snippet: I actually looked into this a few weeks ago when I saw the renewal was coming up...
subject: Re: Home warranty renewal

body:
Hey, saw the society thread about staging the show display this weekend. Are you actually planning to haul the cattleyas down to the convention center Saturday night? Feels like really bad timing with your mom coming over Sunday morning. Just want to make sure you're not going to be up until 2am again like last year fussing over the staging.

Also, do you need me to keep Emma busy for a few hours so you can pack the plants without interruptions? Let me know what the plan looks like.
snippet: Hey, saw the thread about staging the display this weekend. Are you actually...
subject: Orchid show staging weekend

body:
Ha, yes it's Saturday but we're starting at 6pm and the judging setup window closes at 9, so worst case I'm done by then. I'm way more organized this time — I've labeled every pot and crate so I can stage section by section instead of all at once.

Taking Emma out would actually be amazing. Even just for the afternoon so I can wrap and pack the six entries without interruptions. Maybe 2-6pm? I'll owe you big.
snippet: Ha, yes it's Saturday but we're starting at 6pm and judging setup closes at 9...
subject: Re: Orchid show staging weekend

body:
Done. I'll take Emma to the art museum and we'll grab dinner after, so you'll have the house until at least 7. That gives you plenty of buffer before you head down to stage.

Good luck with it. And please actually sleep before your mom gets here — she will absolutely notice if you're running on fumes again.

Craig
snippet: Done. I'll take her to the art museum and we'll grab dinner after, so you'll...
subject: Re: Orchid show staging weekend
